two_body_problem.title: Proper Horizontal Offset
two_body_problem.principle: Place the red ball to the left of the green ball to ensure a horizontal push towards the blue ball.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball needs to push the green ball horizontally towards the blue ball.

two_body_problem.title: Increase Radius for Stronger Push
two_body_problem.principle: Increasing the radius of the red ball increases its mass and the force of the push.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When a stronger push is needed to ensure the green ball contacts the blue ball.

two_body_problem.title: Optimal Y-Coordinate for Red Ball
two_body_problem.principle: Placing the red ball at a y-coordinate around 3.5 provides a balanced initial push.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball needs to provide an initial push to the green ball.

two_body_problem.title: Adjust Y-Coordinate for Initial Velocity
two_body_problem.principle: Raising the y-coordinate of the red ball increases its gravitational potential energy, providing a stronger initial push.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When a stronger initial push is needed to ensure the green ball contacts the blue ball.

two_body_problem.title: Ensure Non-Overlap with Green and Blue Balls
two_body_problem.principle: Place the red ball such that it does not overlap with the green or blue balls to avoid invalid placement.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: Whenever placing the red ball to ensure it does not overlap with other balls.
two_body_problem.example: Move at least 0.5713 units further away from the green ball.

two_body_problem.description: Agent repeatedly fails to place the red ball far enough from the green ball.
two_body_problem.why_it_happens: Misunderstanding the required horizontal distance for non-overlap.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Use the compute_relative_positions tool to calculate the correct distance.

two_body_problem.description: Agent attempts to place the red ball too close to the right wall, leading to invalid placements.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Ensure the red ball is placed within the valid range of the world bounds.

two_body_problem.description: Agent fails to recognize that moving the red ball horizontally does not guarantee non-overlap with the green ball.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Use the compute_relative_positions tool to check for overlap in both dimensions.

two_body_problem.description: Agent places the red ball too low, causing it to be out of bounds and invalid.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Ensure the y-coordinate of the red ball is within the valid range.
